message:
refactor(rch): convert bail!() calls to structured errors (bd-1nk5)

Replace remaining bail!() calls in rch/src with structured error types:

- fleet/ssh.rs: Use FleetError::UnexpectedOutput for df parsing errors
- fleet/executor.rs: Use SshError::ConnectionFailed and FleetError::HealthCheckFailed
- fleet/rollback.rs: Use FleetError::NoBackupAvailable and FleetError::RollbackFailed
- fleet/mod.rs: Use BinaryError::NotFound for missing worker binary
- agent/hook.rs: Add HookError::NotSupported (E506) and NotImplemented (E507)
- completions.rs: Use CompletionError::UnsupportedShell

Also renumbers UpdateError codes from E506-E510 to E520-E524 to avoid
conflicts with new HookError variants.

All 1706 tests pass. No bail!() calls remain in rch/src.

Note: rch-common, rch-wkr, and rch-telemetry still have bail!() calls
that will be addressed in a follow-up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1mzg","title":"Test: Hook Classification Timing Budget (<1ms/<5ms)","description":"## Purpose
Test that hook classification decisions meet the strict timing budgets documented in AGENTS.md.

## Timing Budgets (from AGENTS.md)
- Non-compilation rejection: <1ms, panic at 5ms
- Compilation acceptance: <5ms, panic at 10ms

## Test Cases

### Non-Compilation Commands (<1ms)
1. Simple commands:
   - `ls`, `echo hello`, `pwd`
   - Measure: time from input to "allow local" decision
   - Assert: < 1ms for 99th percentile

2. Git commands:
   - `git status`, `git diff`, `git log`
   - Assert: < 1ms (NEVER_INTERCEPT fast path)

3. Docker commands:
   - `docker ps`, `docker build`
   - Assert: < 1ms (NEVER_INTERCEPT fast path)

### Compilation Commands (<5ms)
4. Cargo commands:
   - `cargo build`, `cargo test`, `cargo check`
   - Measure: time to classify and decide to offload
   - Assert: < 5ms for 99th percentile

5. C compiler commands:
   - `gcc`, `g++`, `clang`
   - Assert: < 5ms classification

6. Complex commands:
   - Long command strings with many arguments
   - Assert: still < 5ms

### Statistical Validation
7. Run 1000 iterations:
   - Calculate mean, p50, p95, p99
   - Assert: p99 < budget
   - Assert: variance is low (stable performance)

## Measurement Method
```rust
let start = Instant::now();
let decision = classify_command(cmd);
let elapsed = start.elapsed();
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Non-compilation: p99 < 1ms
- Compilation: p99 < 5ms
- No single measurement exceeds panic threshold
- Low variance across runs

## MANDATORY Logging

All tests MUST use TestLogger with structured JSON output:

```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_classification_timing","phase":"setup","msg":"Preparing benchmark","data":{"iterations":1000,"warmup":100}}
{"ts":"...","level":"DEBUG","test":"test_classification_timing","phase":"measure","msg":"Single measurement","data":{"cmd":"ls","decision":"allow_local","elapsed_us":47}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_classification_timing","phase":"stats","msg":"Non-compilation stats","data":{"cmd_type":"simple","n":1000,"mean_us":52,"p50_us":48,"p95_us":89,"p99_us":156,"max_us":312}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_classification_timing","phase":"stats","msg":"Compilation stats","data":{"cmd_type":"cargo","n":1000,"mean_us":1847,"p50_us":1623,"p95_us":3412,"p99_us":4156,"max_us":5892}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_classification_timing","phase":"verify","msg":"Timing budget check","data":{"category":"non_compilation","p99_us":156,"budget_us":1000,"within_budget":true}}
{"ts":"...","level":"WARN","test":"test_classification_timing","phase":"verify","msg":"Approaching panic threshold","data":{"category":"compilation","max_us":9500,"panic_threshold_us":10000}}
```

### Required Logging Points
1. Benchmark setup (iterations, warmup count)
2. Individual measurements (DEBUG level for each iteration)
3. Statistical summary per command category (mean, p50, p95, p99, max)
4. Budget verification (within/exceeds budget)
5. Warning when approaching panic thresholds
6. Clear PASS/FAIL verdict with all percentile data","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:26:48.056493826Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:30:49.411612805Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T00:30:49.411336083Z","close_reason":"Merged into bd-1nhd (Performance & Timing Budget Tests).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mzg","depends_on_id":"bd-17tg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:27:03.805578424Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1mzg","depends_on_id":"bd-1nhd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:27:01.972845620Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1nhd","title":"True E2E Performance & Timing Budget Tests","description":"# Feature: True E2E Performance & Timing Budget Tests

## Purpose

Test that rch meets its strict performance budgets documented in AGENTS.md to ensure it doesn't slow down AI agent workflows.

## MANDATORY Logging Requirements

Performance tests MUST use structured JSON logging with precise timing:

### Performance-Specific Log Events

```json
// Timing measurement
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_hook_classification_ls","phase":"measure","msg":"Classification timing","data":{"cmd":"ls -la","classification":"non_compile","tier":2,"elapsed_us":234,"budget_us":1000,"within_budget":true}}

// Budget check
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_hook_classification_ls","phase":"verify","msg":"Budget verification","data":{"metric":"hook_decision","measured_us":234,"budget_us":1000,"panic_threshold_us":5000,"status":"pass"}}

// Statistical summary
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_hook_classification","phase":"stats","msg":"Timing statistics","data":{"samples":100,"mean_us":245,"p50_us":230,"p95_us":380,"p99_us":520,"max_us":890,"stddev_us":87}}

// Full pipeline overhead
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_pipeline_overhead","phase":"measure","msg":"Pipeline timing","data":{"local_ms":5432,"remote_ms":5890,"overhead_ms":458,"overhead_pct":8.4,"budget_pct":15,"status":"pass"}}

// Panic threshold alert
{"ts":"...","level":"WARN","test":"test_hook_panic","phase":"verify","msg":"Approaching panic threshold","data":{"measured_us":4200,"panic_threshold_us":5000,"headroom_pct":16}}
```

### Timing Metrics to Log

1. **Microsecond precision**: for sub-millisecond budgets
2. **Statistical summary**: mean, p50, p95, p99, max, stddev
3. **Budget comparison**: measured vs budget vs panic threshold
4. **Overhead calculation**: local baseline, remote total, overhead %
5. **Sample count**: number of measurements for statistical validity

## Why This Matters

- EVERY Bash command passes through the rch hook
- Slow hook = slow agent = frustrated users
- AGENTS.md specifies hard timing budgets:
  - Hook decision (non-compilation): <1ms, panic at 5ms
  - Hook decision (compilation): <5ms, panic at 10ms
  - Worker selection: <10ms, panic at 50ms
  - Full pipeline: <15% overhead, panic at 50%

## Performance Scenarios to Test

### Hook Classification Performance

1. **Non-compilation command rejection (<1ms)**
   - `ls -la` - Log: classification time, tier, decision
   - `git status` - Log: must not hit compilation path
   - `echo hello` - Log: shell builtin handling
   
2. **Compilation command acceptance (<5ms)**
   - `cargo build` - Log: tier 4 classification time
   - `cargo test` - Log: argument parsing time
   - `gcc hello.c` - Log: C compiler detection time
   
3. **Complex command classification**
   - Long command strings - Log: scaling with length
   - Commands with many arguments - Log: arg parse time
   - Piped commands - Log: should be rejected quickly

### Worker Selection Performance

1. **Single worker selection (<10ms)**
   - Log: selection algorithm time, worker chosen
   
2. **Multi-worker selection with scoring**
   - Log: scoring time per worker, total time
   
3. **Selection with cache affinity matching**
   - Log: cache lookup time, affinity bonus
   
4. **Selection under heavy load** (all slots busy)
   - Log: queue time, slot wait if any

### Full Pipeline Overhead

1. **Small project (< 1000 files)**: overhead < 15%
   - Log: file count, sync time, compile time, total overhead
   
2. **Medium project (< 10000 files)**: overhead < 15%
   - Log: sync scaling, compile scaling
   
3. **Large project (> 50000 files)**: overhead < 15%
   - Log: detailed phase timing breakdown
   
4. **Incremental builds (cached)**: minimal overhead
   - Log: cache hit rate, reduced sync time

### Streaming Performance

1. **Output streaming latency**
   - Log: first byte time, steady state throughput
   
2. **Large output handling (>1MB)**
   - Log: buffering behavior, memory usage
   
3. **Progress indicator responsiveness**
   - Log: update frequency, display latency

## Test Methodology

```rust
pub struct TimingTest {
    samples: Vec<Duration>,
    budget: Duration,
    panic_threshold: Duration,
    logger: TestLogger,  // MANDATORY
}

impl TimingTest {
    /// Run timing test N times, log each measurement
    pub fn measure_n(&mut self, n: usize, f: impl Fn()) {
        for i in 0..n {
            let start = Instant::now();
            f();
            let elapsed = start.elapsed();
            self.samples.push(elapsed);
            self.logger.timing(i, elapsed, self.budget);
        }
    }
    
    /// Calculate and log statistics
    pub fn summarize(&mut self) -> TimingStats {
        let stats = compute_stats(&self.samples);
        self.logger.stats(stats, self.budget, self.panic_threshold);
        stats
    }
}
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] ALL timing budgets met under normal conditions
- [ ] Panic thresholds never exceeded
- [ ] Performance stable across multiple runs (low variance)
- [ ] No regression from baseline measurements
- [ ] ALL tests emit structured JSON logs with timing
- [ ] Statistical summaries logged for every performance test
- [ ] Measurements include p95/p99 percentiles

## Technical Notes

- Use std::time::Instant for precise timing
- Run tests multiple times (N >= 100) for statistical significance
- Test on both cold and warm caches
- Document baseline measurements for comparison
- Log system info (CPU, load average) for context

## Dependencies

- Requires bd-3saj (test infrastructure for timing measurement)
- Requires bd-12hi (cargo compilation for full pipeline tests)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"HazySparrow","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:21:35.417435322Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T23:31:54.183463187Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T23:31:54.183390281Z","close_reason":"Completed true-e2e performance & timing budget tests (hook decision + selection + consistency)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nhd","depends_on_id":"bd-12hi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:08:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nhd","depends_on_id":"bd-1cwg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:08:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nhd","depends_on_id":"bd-3saj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:08:27Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"bd-1nhd","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation complete: Created tests/true_e2e/performance_tests.rs with 6 tests covering timing budgets. Tests verify non-compilation (<1ms), compilation (<5ms), complex commands, worker selection, accuracy, and consistency. All tests pass.","created_at":"2026-01-26T23:10:00Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1nk5","title":"EPIC: Error Handling and User Messaging","description":"# Error Handling and User Messaging

## Overview
RCH has an excellent error infrastructure using miette for rich diagnostics. However, not all error paths use it consistently, leading to user-unfriendly error messages in some cases.

## Current State

### Strong Points
- Proper error types in rch/src/error.rs with miette Diagnostic
- Error codes follow RCH-Exxx convention
- Rich help text with remediation suggestions

### Problems Found
There are ~35 `bail\!()` calls in commands.rs that bypass the structured error system:

```rust
// Line 2167 - No error code, no context, no remediation
bail\!("rch-wkr not found on remote");

// Line 2197 - Same problem  
bail\!("rustup install failed: {}", stderr.trim());

// Line 2943 - Generic error
bail\!("rustup install failed: {}", stderr.trim());
```

These plain string errors:
1. Have no error code for scripting/automation
2. Provide no remediation steps
3. Don't indicate which worker/host failed
4. Can't be programmatically parsed

## Error Code Gaps
Current codes have gaps suggesting incomplete coverage:
- E001-E004: Config (has E005-E006 gap)
- E007-E009: Worker 
- E100-E108: SSH (has gaps)
- E202, E204, E207: Worker health (missing E203, E205, E206)
- No E300+ category defined for build errors

## Key Files
- rch/src/error.rs - Error type definitions
- rch/src/commands.rs - Where most bail\!() calls live
- rch-common/src/ui/error.rs - ErrorPanel display

## Success Criteria
- Every user-facing error has an RCH-Exxx code
- Every error includes actionable remediation steps
- Error codes are documented in a public reference
- No bare bail\!() calls without structured context","status":"in_progress","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:58:56.426351214Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:42:34.248054782Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":108,"issue_id":"bd-1nk5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Phase 1 complete (VioletBarn): Added PlatformError (E600-E603), BinaryError (E800-E803), FleetError (E700-E704) to rch/src/error.rs. Updated test ranges. Next: Convert bail!() calls to use these types.","created_at":"2026-01-30T22:42:34Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1nmv","title":"Add configurable external timeout for all remote commands to prevent zombie processes","description":"# Feature: Configurable External Timeout Protection

## Background

Per user request, extend the bun test timeout protection to ALL remote commands to prevent zombie/stuck/misbehaving processes from bringing down worker machines.

## Current State

- bd-15ma added hardcoded 600s timeout for BunTest/BunTypecheck commands
- Other command types (cargo test, cargo build, gcc, etc.) have no external timeout protection
- SSH command_timeout exists but may not work for CPU-bound stuck processes

## Proposed Solution

1. Add configurable timeout settings to CompilationConfig:
   - `default_external_timeout_sec: u64` (default: 3600 = 1 hour)
   - `test_external_timeout_sec: u64` (default: 1800 = 30 minutes)
   - `bun_external_timeout_sec: u64` (default: 600 = 10 minutes, per known issues)

2. Modify `wrap_with_external_timeout()` to:
   - Apply to ALL commands, not just bun
   - Use appropriate timeout based on CompilationKind
   - Add config option to disable (`external_timeout_enabled: bool`)

3. Add monitoring/logging:
   - Log when timeout kills a process (exit code 137 = SIGKILL)
   - Track timeout events in telemetry

## Benefits

- Prevents runaway processes from consuming worker slots indefinitely
- Protects against various stuck scenarios (infinite loops, deadlocks, resource contention)
- Configurable per command type for flexibility","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-30T19:28:26.248490139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T20:46:07.910883617Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T20:46:07.909259266Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+        help(
+            "Expected to find the web dashboard at one of:\n  \
+            - ./web\n  \
+            - ~/.local/share/rch/web\n  \
+            - /usr/local/share/rch/web\n\n\
+            If you installed from source, run 'rch web' from the project root."
+        )
+    )]
+    DashboardNotFound,
+
+    /// Web server exited with error.
+    #[error("Web server exited with error (exit code: {exit_code:?})")]
+    #[diagnostic(
+        code("RCH-E901"),
+        help("Check the server logs for details. The web server may have encountered a startup error.")
+    )]
+    ServerExitedWithError { exit_code: Option<i32> },
+
+    /// Web server failed to start.
+    #[error("Failed to start web server: {reason}")]
+    #[diagnostic(
+        code("RCH-E902"),
+        help("Ensure bun or npm is installed and the web dependencies are available")
+    )]
+    ServerStartFailed { reason: String },
+}
+
+// =============================================================================
+// Completion Errors
+// =============================================================================
+
+/// Errors related to shell completion functionality.
+///
+/// Error code range: RCH-E910 to RCH-E915
+#[derive(Error, Diagnostic, Debug)]
+pub enum CompletionError {
+    /// Shell not supported for completion installation.
+    #[error("Shell '{shell}' is not supported for automatic completion installation")]
+    #[diagnostic(
+        code("RCH-E910"),
+        help(
+            "Supported shells for automatic installation: bash, zsh, fish, elvish, powershell\n\n\
+            For other shells, generate completions manually:\n  \
+            rch completions <shell> > ~/.rch-completions"
+        )
+    )]
+    UnsupportedShell { shell: String },
+
+    /// Completion file write failed.
+    #[error("Failed to write completion file: {path}")]
+    #[diagnostic(
+        code("RCH-E911"),
+        help("Check that you have write permissions to the completion directory")
+    )]
+    WriteFailed {
+        path: String,
+        #[source]
+        source: std::io::Error,
+    },
+}
+
+// =============================================================================
+// Editor Errors
+// =============================================================================
+
+/// Errors related to external editor operations.
+///
+/// Error code range: RCH-E920 to RCH-E925
+#[derive(Error, Diagnostic, Debug)]
+pub enum EditorError {
+    /// Editor exited with non-zero status.
+    #[error("Editor exited with non-zero status (exit code: {exit_code:?})")]
+    #[diagnostic(
+        code("RCH-E920"),
+        help("The editor process exited with an error. Check that your $EDITOR is configured correctly.")
+    )]
+    ExitedWithError { exit_code: Option<i32> },
+
+    /// Failed to launch editor.
+    #[error("Failed to launch editor '{editor}'")]
+    #[diagnostic(
+        code("RCH-E921"),
+        help("Ensure your $EDITOR environment variable points to a valid editor")
+    )]
+    LaunchFailed {
+        editor: String,
+        #[source]
+        source: std::io::Error,
+    },
+}
